9. Lie Groups

9.1. Theory

Definition 5 (Working defintionof a manifold and tangent space). A smooth manifold locally looks like . At every point we have a tangent space , defined via curves8:

(42)

[TODO] Just view abstractly?

We denote the tangent space at the identity as . Also, define , which contains all matrices, since is well defined for all .

Definition 6 (Lie Groups). A Lie group is a smooth manifold that is also a group, such that the multiplication and inversion are smooth maps.
Definition 7 (One Parameter Subgroup). A one-parameter subgroup is a smooth homomorphism .

Characterization of one-parameter subgroups

Differentiating the homomorphism condition wrt s at yields

(43)

( represents the velocity of a curve passing through the origin and hence it belongs the tangent space)

By ODE existence and uniqueness (which applies because is a smooth manifold), the solution is an exponential:

(44)

Hence a one-parameter subgroup is parameterized by , and we can define the exponential map: that outputs the group element

(45)

you reach be following the one-parameter subgroup with initial velocity for time .

Why did we introduce one-parameter subgroups?

  • For compact connected Lie groups, every group element lies on some one-parameter subgroup, so we can specify each group element can be written as for some , i.e. just coordinates .
  • They provide a bridge to representations via the functoriality theorem.

9.1.1. Representations and Generators

Definition 8.

  • A Lie group homomorphism is a smooth group homomorphism.
  • A representation of a Lie group is a Lie group homomorphism .

Consider a Lie group representation . A curve through in with velocity after applying becomes a curve through in . Define the pushforward which maps the velocity to the velocity:

(46)

(Recall contains matrices, and the argument of right hand side is just a matrix-valued function of )

Definition 9 (Generator associated with representation). Now pick a basis of of . The generators wrt are .

Theorem 5 (Naturality of the exponential map). Let be a Lie group homomorphism, and let be its derivative at the identity. Then we have a commutation diagram relation: The exponential map is related to any Lie group homomorphism in the following way.

(47)

The right column is a matrix exponential. The left column is the exponential map.

9.4. Finding irreps

The idea is: start with some known representations, form tensor products, then decompose those products into irreducibles. If your starting reps are rich enough, every irrep eventually shows up this way. This works for compact groups by Theorem of the highest weight?

Fact 1 (Peter-Weyl). For a compact group , the matrix coefficients of all

9.5.1. Class stuff

Fact 2 (Consequence of Maximal Torus Theorem plus Naturality). If is compact and connected, then is surjective. For matrix groups, the two columns of the commutative diagram are the same, and hence every matrix in that group can be represented as the exponential of some other matrix.

Elements of SO(n) are orthogonal matrices with determine 1. Hence elements of are already matrices, and is the identity since is the identity (plug into Equation 46)

Proposition 7. The generators of SO(n) are skew-symmetric matrices, i.e. every matrix in SO(n) is the exponential of some other matrix.

The proof takes two strategies. Since by Fact 2 we know every element of is the exponential of some matrix, we can write it out and obtain that such generators are skew-symmetric. Alternatively, we can consider a curve and apply the constraint . The dimension of the Lie Algebra is thus .

9.6. Algorithms on Generators

Change of basis, similarity, direct sum, and tensor product can be applied on the generators.

  • For example, by the naturality theorem, finding a change of basis between two representations suffices to finding a change of basis between the generators.

    • Same thing for a similarity transform.

  • Generator of the direct sum is a block diagonal matrix per generator:

  • Generator of the tensor product is the kronecker sum of the generators.

    Explanation
  • Finding irreps: find stuff that commutes with the generators since exponential of generators is just powers of the generators.
